resources.name: About this list (litigation funding)
resources.description: Some directory members are brokers, marketplaces, advisers, or law firms—not direct litigation funders. For those, the notes describe types of matters they say they help place, advise on, or support, rather than that they fund every case type. Entries for Augusta, Innsworth, Orchard Global, and Vannin are conservative where a clear public “what we fund” list could not be verified from accessible sources.

resources.name: Asertis
resources.description: Manchester, England. Areas of law / claim types: insolvency, commercial litigation, arbitration, group actions / collective redress, securities litigation, and asset tracing / enforcement.

resources.name: Augusta
resources.description: London SW1V, England. Areas of law / claim types (from augustaventures.com): litigation and arbitration funding for lawyers, businesses, and individuals; consumer and commercial disputes across multiple jurisdictions; emphasis on complex claims, specialist sector teams, and claims promoting ESG-related causes.

resources.name: Bench Walk Advisors
resources.description: London EC4M, England. Areas of law / claim types: international and commercial arbitration, insolvency, class actions, global litigation matters, portfolio arrangements, and defence funding.

resources.name: Balance Legal Capital
resources.description: London WC2A, England. Areas of law / claim types: commercial litigation, IP disputes, collective / class actions, competition-style group claims, and funded business claims.

resources.name: Burford Capital
resources.description: London EC4M, England. Areas of law / claim types: commercial litigation, arbitration, contract, fraud, fiduciary duty, securities, antitrust / competition, intellectual property, insolvency / bankruptcy, and insurance recovery.

resources.name: Calunius Capital
resources.description: London SW1Y, England. Areas of law / claim types: large-scale commercial litigation and arbitration, including domestic and international disputes.

resources.name: Deminor Litigation Funding
resources.description: London WC1X, England. Areas of law / claim types: business claims, commercial disputes, arbitration, investment recovery, group antitrust actions, and individual B2B claims.

resources.name: Erso Capital
resources.description: London EC3M, England. Areas of law / claim types: commercial and contractual disputes, business torts, insolvency / bankruptcy claims, patent disputes, domestic and international arbitration, investment treaty arbitration, class actions / group litigation, antitrust / competition, mass torts, and ESG matters.

resources.name: Harbour
resources.description: London SW1Y, England. Areas of law / claim types: high-value commercial disputes, litigation, and arbitration; also single-claim and portfolio funding.

resources.name: Innsworth
resources.description: London WC2A, England. Areas of law / claim types (from innsworth.com): commercial litigation and arbitration above US$100m; shareholder group claims; class/collective redress (including UK Competition Appeal Tribunal work); claims by administrators and insolvency practitioners; investor–state arbitrations. Site lists funded examples spanning competition, securities/shareholder, GDPR/collective privacy, and cross-border treaty disputes.

resources.name: Omni Bridgeway
resources.description: London WC2A, England. Areas of law / claim types: commercial disputes, breach of contract, breach of fiduciary duty, complex business disputes, environmental claims, antitrust, arbitration, construction disputes, judgments / award enforcement, class actions, and broader commercial litigation.

resources.name: Redress Solutions
resources.description: London EC2M, England. Areas of law / claim types: commercial claims, business-owner claims, insolvency-related claims, and law-firm portfolios / funded client claims.

resources.name: Therium
resources.description: London WC1V, England. Areas of law / claim types: commercial litigation, arbitration, portfolio funding, securities group litigation, equal pay / employment-style group claims, and large group claimant cases.

resources.name: Winward Litigation Finance
resources.description: London WC2A, England. Areas of law / claim types: primarily commercial disputes in litigation and arbitration, including contractual and tortious disputes, plus meritorious group claims.

resources.name: Woodsford
resources.description: London WC1B, England. Areas of law / claim types: collective redress, shareholder / investor matters, ESG-related matters, and broader funded claimant-side litigation.

resources.name: AxiaFunder
resources.description: London W6, England. Areas of law / claim types: commercial litigation, class actions, and housing disrepair claims; generally claimant-side funded litigation through its platform.

resources.name: ClaimTrading
resources.description: London SW1A, England. Role: broker / arranger for litigation and arbitration funding, including claim prosecution, enforcement of judgments / awards, portfolio finance, and upfront monetisation.

resources.name: Factor Risk Management
resources.description: London EC3N, England. Role: adviser / broker on litigation funding and ATE insurance rather than a direct funder; supports civil and commercial litigation matters and related risk/insurance structures.

resources.name: Mourant Ozannes
resources.description: St Peter Port, Guernsey. Role: law firm, not a litigation funder; litigation/dispute work includes offshore disputes, fund disputes, investor claims, redemption issues, governance/director liability, valuation disputes, and insolvency / fund wind-ups.

resources.name: QLP
resources.description: London EC3V, England. Role: broker for ATE insurance and litigation funding; pages reference commercial disputes, insolvency-related court-fee funding, and clinical negligence / personal injury matters.

resources.name: Sentry Funding
resources.description: London WC1V, England. Role: marketplace / panel access, not a single named funder; says it matches claimants to legal finance tailored to the case type, value, and jurisdiction.
